reasoning: Both getClientIp and mw are explicitly exported via module.exports and are the primary public API. The mw() middleware requires no options (options defaults to {}) and its only accepted option (attributeName) controls only the property name attached to req, not header trust behavior. The vulnerable code path — reading attacker-controlled headers (x-client-ip, x-forwarded-for, cf-connecting-ip, fastly-client-ip, true-client-ip, x-real-ip, x-cluster-client-ip, x-forwarded, forwarded-for, forwarded, x-appengine-user-ip, Cf-Pseudo-IPv4) in strict priority order before TCP-level sources — executes on every single request with zero configuration. The only check performed is is.ip() which validates that the header value is syntactically a valid IP address; it does not verify the upstream sender is a trusted proxy. There is no trusted-proxy allowlist, no isTrustedProxy mechanism, no proxyList, and no TRUSTED_PROXIES concept anywhere in the codebase. Any remote attacker who can send an HTTP request can set X-Client-IP (or any of the other checked headers) to a syntactically valid spoofed IP and the library will return that value as the authoritative client IP. No non-default options are required to trigger the vulnerability. Severity is kept at medium (not raised) because exploitation requires the caller to use the returned IP for access control or rate-limiting, and deployments behind a properly-configured reverse proxy that strips/overwrites these headers before they reach the application are not affected.
